nodes.name: withIsolatedShapes
nodes.description:
Run `callback` in a world where all bindings from the shapes in `shapeIds` to shapes not in
`shapeIds` are removed. This is useful when you want to duplicate/copy shapes without worrying
about bindings that might be pointing to shapes that are not being duplicated.

The callback is given the set of bindings that should be maintained.

nodes.name: FocusManager
nodes.description:
A manager for ensuring correct focus across the editor.
It will listen for changes in the instance state to make sure the
container is focused when the editor is focused.
Also, it will make sure that the focus is on things like text
labels when the editor is in editing mode.

nodes.name: TldrawRBush
nodes.description: Custom RBush class for tldraw shapes.

nodes.name: RBushIndex
nodes.description:
Wrapper around RBush R-tree for efficient spatial queries.
Maintains a map of elements currently in the tree for efficient updates.

nodes.name: SpatialIndexManager
nodes.description:
Manages spatial indexing for efficient shape location queries.

Uses an R-tree (via RBush) to enable O(log n) spatial queries instead of O(n) iteration.
Handles shapes with computed bounds (arrows, groups, custom shapes) by checking all shapes'
bounds on each update using the reactive bounds cache.

Key features:
- Incremental updates using filterHistory pattern
- Leverages existing bounds cache reactivity for dependency tracking
- Works with any custom shape type with computed bounds
- Per-page index (rebuilds on page change)
- Optimized for viewport culling queries

nodes.name: useSvgExportContext
nodes.description:
Returns the current SVG export context. Returns null if the component isn't being rendered for an
SVG export.

nodes.name: useDelaySvgExport
nodes.description:
Delay an SVG export until the returned function is called. This is useful if e.g. your shape
loads data dynamically, and you need to prevent the export from happening until after the data is
loaded.

If used outside of an SVG export, this hook has no effect.
examples:
```tsx
const readyForExport = useDelaySvgExport()

return <MyDynamicComponent onDataLoaded={() => readyForExport()} />
```

nodes.name: ExportDelay
nodes.description:
Export delay is a helper class that allows you to wait for a set of promises to resolve before
proceeding with an export. Over time, promises can be added by calling `waitUntil`.

When `resolve` is called, we'll wait for all the promises already added (and any new ones added
in the mean time) to resolve before proceeding. The class is designed to be used once: after
`resolve` has been called and finished, new promises cannot be added.

nodes.name: FontEmbedder
nodes.description:
Because SVGs cannot refer to external CSS/font resources, any web fonts used in the SVG must be
embedded as data URLs in inlined

nodes.name: measureContentBounds
nodes.description:
Scans a canvas from each edge inward (up to trimPaddingPx pixels) to find
the first row/column containing non-background content. Returns the crop
rectangle in canvas pixel coordinates, or null if no trimming is needed.
nodes.returnType: { cropLeft: number; cropTop: number; cropRight: number; cropBottom: number } | null

nodes.name: trimExtraPadding
nodes.description:
Trims extra padding from a canvas by scanning from each edge inward to find
non-transparent (or non-background) pixels. Stops at either content pixels or
the declared bounds (the area without extra padding).
nodes.returnType: { canvas: HTMLCanvasElement; width: number; height: number }

nodes.name: trimSvgToContent
nodes.description:
Trims an SVG string to its visual content bounds by rendering it to a
temporary canvas, measuring the actual content area, then adjusting the
SVG's viewBox and dimensions to match.

structuredParams.name: options

nodes.returnType: Promise<{ svg: string; width: number; height: number } | null>

nodes.name: getExportDefaultBounds
nodes.description:
Calculates the default bounds for an SVG export. This function handles:
1. Computing masked page bounds for each shape
2. Container logic: if a shape is marked as an export bounds container and it
   contains all other shapes, use its bounds and skip padding
3. Otherwise, create a union of all shape bounds and apply padding

The container logic is useful for cases like annotating on an image - if the image
contains all annotations, we want to export exactly the image bounds without extra padding.

structuredParams.name: renderingShapes
structuredParams.description: The shapes to include in the export

structuredParams.name: padding
structuredParams.description: Padding to add around the bounds (only applied if no container bounds)

structuredParams.name: singleFrameShapeId
structuredParams.description: If exporting a single frame, this is its ID (skips padding)

nodes.returnType: { box: Box; paddingApplied: boolean } | { box: null; paddingApplied: false }

nodes.name: tlenvReactive
nodes.description:
An atom that contains information about the current device and environment.
This object is reactive and will update automatically when the environment changes.
Use it for values that may change over time, such as the pointer type.

nodes.name: useEvent
nodes.description:
Allows you to define event handlers that can read the latest props/state but has a stable
function identity.

These event callbacks may not be called in React render functions! An error won't be thrown, but
in the real implementation it would be!

Uses a modified version of the user-land implementation included in the [`useEvent()` RFC][1].
Our version until such a hook is available natively.

The RFC was closed on 27 September 2022, the React team plans to come up with a new RFC to
provide similar functionality in the future. We will migrate to this functionality when
available.

IMPORTANT CAVEAT: You should not call event callbacks in layout effects of React component
children! Internally this hook uses a layout effect and parent component layout effects run after
child component layout effects. Use this hook responsibly.

[1]: https://github.com/reactjs/rfcs/pull/220

nodes.name: useReactiveEvent
nodes.description:
/**
 * like {@link useEvent}, but for use in reactive contexts - when the handler function changes, it
 * will invalidate any reactive contexts that call it.
 *

nodes.name: useFixSafariDoubleTapZoomPencilEvents

When double tapping with the pencil in iOS, it enables a little zoom window in the UI. We don't
want this for drawing operations and can disable it by setting 'disableDoubleTapZoom' in the main
editor.

nodes.name: useActivePeerIds$
nodes.description:
Returns a computed signal of active peer user IDs that should be shown.
Automatically re-evaluates on an interval to handle time-based state transitions
(active -> idle -> inactive).

nodes.name: useRefState
nodes.description:
A `useState` that uses a `ref` to keep the state in sync with the last value assigned to it.

This is useful when creating side-effect state that behave correctly in react strict mode. The
obvious way to do this is to use `useState` within a `useEffect` like this:

```ts
const [state, setState] = useState(null)
useEffect(() => {
  const thing = createSideEffectThing()
  setState(thing)
  return () => thing.destroy()
}, [deps])
```

The problem with this is that when initially mounting in strict mode, react will:
- Call the initial effect and set state with an instance
- Call the cleanup function and destroy the instance
- Call the effect again and set state with a new instance
- Restore the state to the first instance

Now, our effect and our state are out of sync: the effect is using the new instance, but the
state contains the old instance which has been destroyed.

Using a `ref` is a solution, as it'll always keep the value last assigned to it, so it stays in
sync with the effect. That's no good for rendering though, as react won't trigger a re-render
when the contents of the ref changes.

This hook solves this problem by using a `ref` to keep the value in sync with the effect, and
a `useState` to trigger a re-render when the value changes.

nodes.name: useUniqueSafeId
nodes.description:
React's useId hook returns a unique id for the component. However, it uses a colon in the id,
which is not valid for CSS selectors. This hook replaces the colon with an underscore.

nodes.name: useSharedSafeId

React's useId hook returns a unique id for the component. However, it uses a colon in the id,

nodes.name: preventDefault
nodes.description:
This function calls `event.preventDefault()` for you. Why is that useful?

Because if you enable `window.preventDefaultLogging = true` it'll log out a message when it
happens. Because we use console.warn rather than (log) you'll get a stack trace in the inspector
telling you exactly where it happened. This is important because `e.preventDefault()` is the
source of many bugs, but unfortunately it can't be avoided because it also stops a lot of default
behaviour which doesn't make sense in our UI

Subtract the range b from the range a. If b is completely inside a, return the two ranges of a

nodes.name: getBoundShapeRelationships
nodes.description:
Get the relationships for an arrow that has two bound shape terminals.
If the arrow has only one bound shape, then it is always "safe" to apply
standard offsets and precision behavior. If the shape is bound to the same
shape on both ends, then that is an exception. If one of the shape's
terminals is bound to a shape that contains / is contained by the shape that
is bound to the other terminal, then that is also an exception.

structuredParams.name: endShapeId
structuredParams.description: the bound shape from the arrow's end

nodes.name: clampArrowTerminalToMask
nodes.description:
If the arrow terminal point falls outside the bound shape's mask (e.g. when a shape
extends beyond a frame boundary and is clipped), clamp the terminal to the mask boundary.
Uses the binding anchor point (inside the shape/frame) as the ray origin, since the
arrow endpoint may be entirely outside the mask.

Ensures the shape meets minimum size requirements and is square if originally small.

nodes.name: mapToImageCoords
nodes.description: Map a point in shape space to normalized [0,1] image coordinates, accounting for crop and flip.

structuredParams.name: bounds
structuredParams.type: { minX: number; minY: number; w: number; h: number }

nodes.name: isImagePointTransparent
nodes.description: Returns true if the point maps to a transparent pixel. Returns false if alpha data isn't loaded yet.

structuredParams.name: bounds
structuredParams.type: { minX: number; minY: number; w: number; h: number }

nodes.name: preloadAlphaData
nodes.description: Start loading alpha data for a given image URL. No-op if already loaded or loading.

structuredParams.name: url
structuredParams.description: The URL to fetch the image from (may be a resolved/optimized CDN URL).

structuredParams.name: cacheKey
structuredParams.description:
The key to store/lookup the alpha data under. Defaults to `url`.
Pass `asset.props.src` here so that `getAlphaData(asset.props.src)` in getGeometry
finds data that was preloaded from a resolved URL.

nodes.name: getAlphaData
nodes.description: Get cached alpha data for a URL, or null if not yet loaded.

nodes.name: isPointTransparent
nodes.description:
Check whether a point in normalized [0,1] coordinates falls on a transparent pixel.
Returns true if the pixel's alpha is below the threshold.

nodes.name: getCroppedContainerStyle
nodes.description:
When an image is cropped we need to translate the image to show the portion withing the cropped
area. We do this by translating the image by the negative of the top left corner of the crop
area.

structuredParams.name: shape
structuredParams.description: Shape The image shape for which to get the container style

nodes.name: getNoteSizeAdjustments
nodes.description: Get the growY and fontSizeAdjustment for a shape.

nodes.name: getNoteLabelSize
nodes.description: Get the label size for a note.

nodes.name: getNoteAdjacentPositions
nodes.description: Get the adjacent positions for a particular note shape.

structuredParams.name: pagePoint
structuredParams.description: The point of the note shape on the page.

structuredParams.name: pageRotation
structuredParams.description: The rotation of the note shape on the page.

structuredParams.name: growY
structuredParams.description: The growY of the note shape.

structuredParams.name: extraHeight
structuredParams.description: The extra height to add to the top position above the note shape (ie the growY of the dragging shape).

nodes.name: getAvailableNoteAdjacentPositions
nodes.description: Get all of the available note adjacent positions, excluding the selected shapes.

structuredParams.name: rotation
structuredParams.description: The rotation of the note shape.

structuredParams.name: extraHeight
structuredParams.description: The extra height to add to the top position above the note shape (ie the growY of the dragging shape).

nodes.name: getNoteShapeForAdjacentPosition
nodes.description: For a particular adjacent note position, get the shape in that position or create a new one.

structuredParams.name: shape
structuredParams.description: The note shape to create or select.

structuredParams.name: center
structuredParams.description: The center of the note shape.

structuredParams.name: pageRotation
structuredParams.description: The rotation of the note shape on the page.

structuredParams.name: forceNew
structuredParams.description: Whether to force the creation of a new note shape.

nodes.name: PlainTextLabel
nodes.description:
Renders a text label that can be used inside of shapes.
The component has the ability to be edited in place and furthermore
supports rich text editing.

nodes.name: RichTextLabel
nodes.description:
Renders a text label that can be used inside of shapes.
The component has the ability to be edited in place and furthermore
supports rich text editing.

nodes.name: RichTextSVG
nodes.description: Renders a rich text string as SVG given bounds and text properties.

nodes.name: createTextJsxFromSpans
nodes.description: Get an SVG element for a text shape.

nodes.name: getUncroppedSize
nodes.description: Original (uncropped) width and height of shape.

nodes.name: getCropBox
nodes.returnType:
| {
			id: TLShapeId
			type: T['type']
			x: number
			y: number
			props: ShapeWithCrop['props']
	  }
	| undefined

nodes.name: getCroppedImageDataWhenZooming
nodes.description: Calculate new crop dimensions and position when zooming

nodes.name: getCroppedImageDataForReplacedImage
nodes.description: Calculate new crop dimensions and position when replacing an image

nodes.name: getCroppedImageDataForAspectRatio
nodes.description: Calculate new crop dimensions and position when changing aspect ratio

nodes.name: getStroke
nodes.description:
## getStroke

Get an array of points describing a polygon that surrounds the input points.

structuredParams.name: points
structuredParams.description:
An array of points (as `[x, y, pressure]` or `{x, y, pressure}`). Pressure is
optional in both cases.

nodes.name: getStrokeOutlinePoints
nodes.description:
## getStrokeOutlinePoints

Get an array of points (as `[x, y]`) representing the outline of a stroke.

nodes.name: getStrokePoints
nodes.description:
## getStrokePoints

Get an array of points as objects with an adjusted point, pressure, vector, distance, and
runningLength.

nodes.name: getStrokeRadius
nodes.description: Compute a radius based on the pressure.

nodes.name: getSvgPathFromStrokePoints
nodes.description: Turn an array of stroke points into a path of quadratic curves.

structuredParams.name: points
structuredParams.description: The stroke points returned from perfect-freehand

nodes.name: useEfficientZoomThreshold
nodes.description: Returns true when zoomed out far enough that shapes should render in a simplified "solid" style.

nodes.name: useImageOrVideoAsset
nodes.description:
/**
 * This is a handy helper hook that resolves an asset to an optimized URL for a given shape, or its
 * {@link @tldraw/editor#Editor.createTemporaryAssetPreview | placeholder}if the asset is still
 * uploading. This is used in particular for high-resolution images when you want lower and higher
 * resolution depending on the size of the image on the canvas and the zoom level.
 *
 * For image scaling to work, you need to implement scaled URLs in
 * {@link @tldraw/tlschema#TLAssetStore.resolve}.
 *
 *

nodes.name: PlainTextArea
nodes.description: A plain text area that can be used for basic editing text.

nodes.name: RichTextArea
nodes.description:
A rich text area that can be used for editing text with rich text formatting.
This component uses the TipTap editor under the hood.

nodes.name: startEditingShapeWithRichText
nodes.description:
Start editing a shape that has rich text, such as text, note, geo, or arrow shapes.
This will enter the editing state for the shape and optionally select all the text.

structuredParams.name: shapeOrId
structuredParams.description: The shape to start editing. This shape must have a richText property with a TLRichText value.

nodes.name: generateShapeAnnouncementMessage
nodes.description: Core function to generate accessibility announcements for selected shapes

structuredParams.name: args

nodes.name: DefaultRichTextToolbar
nodes.description: The default rich text toolbar.

nodes.name: DefaultRichTextToolbarContent
nodes.description: Rich text toolbar items that have the basics.

nodes.name: DefaultToolbar
nodes.description:
The default toolbar for the editor. `children` defaults to the `DefaultToolbarContent` component.
Depending on the screen size, the children will overflow into a drop-down menu, with the most
recently active item from the overflow being shown in the main toolbar.

nodes.name: TldrawUiContextualToolbar
nodes.description:
A generic floating toolbar that can be used for things
like rich text editing, image toolbars, etc.

nodes.name: TldrawUiSelect
nodes.description: A select dropdown component.
examples:
```tsx
<TldrawUiSelect id="my-select" value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
  <TldrawUiSelectTrigger>
    <TldrawUiSelectValue placeholder="Select..." />
  </TldrawUiSelectTrigger>
  <TldrawUiSelectContent>
    <TldrawUiSelectItem value="one" label="One" />
    <TldrawUiSelectItem value="two" label="Two" />
  </TldrawUiSelectContent>
</TldrawUiSelect>
```

nodes.name: TldrawUiSelectTrigger
nodes.description: The trigger button for the select dropdown.

nodes.name: TldrawUiSelectValue
nodes.description: Displays the currently selected value in the trigger.

nodes.name: TldrawUiSelectContent
nodes.description: The dropdown content container for select items.

nodes.name: TldrawUiSelectItem
nodes.description: An item in the select dropdown. Styled to match TldrawUiMenuCheckboxItem.

nodes.name: TldrawUiRow
nodes.description: A row, usually of UI controls like buttons, select dropdown, checkboxes, etc.

nodes.name: TldrawUiColumn
nodes.description: A column, usually of UI controls like buttons, select dropdown, checkboxes, etc.

nodes.name: TldrawUiGrid
nodes.description:
A tight grid 4 elements wide, usually of UI controls like buttons, select dropdown, checkboxes,
etc.

structuredParams.name: options

nodes.name: pasteFiles
nodes.description: When the clipboard has a file, create an image/video shape from the file and paste it into the scene.

structuredParams.name: point
structuredParams.description: The point at which to paste the file.

nodes.name: pasteUrl
nodes.description:
When the clipboard has plain text that is a valid URL, create a bookmark shape and insert it into
the scene

structuredParams.name: point
structuredParams.description: The point at which to paste the file.

nodes.name: useAnySelectedShapesCount
nodes.description: Returns true if the number of LOCKED OR UNLOCKED selected shapes is at least min or at most max.

nodes.name: useUnlockedSelectedShapesCount
nodes.description: Returns true if the number of UNLOCKED selected shapes is at least min or at most max.

nodes.name: useHasShapesOnPage
nodes.description: Returns true if the current page has at least one shape.

nodes.name: useCanApplySelectionAction
nodes.description:
Returns true if the user is in the select tool and has at least one shape selected.
This corresponds to the `canApplySelectionAction()` check in actions.tsx.

nodes.name: stripHtml
nodes.description: Strip HTML tags from a string.

nodes.name: areShortcutsDisabled
nodes.description: Get whether to disallow clipboard events.

nodes.name: handleText
nodes.description: Handle text pasted into the editor.

structuredParams.name: point
structuredParams.description: The point at which to paste the text.

nodes.name: handlePasteFromEventClipboardData
nodes.description:
Handle a paste using event clipboard data. This is the "original"
paste method that uses the clipboard data from the paste event.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ClipboardEvent/clipboardData

nodes.name: handlePasteFromClipboardApi
nodes.description:
Handle a paste using items retrieved from the Clipboard API.
https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/ClipboardItem

nodes.name: handleNativeOrMenuCopy
nodes.description: When the user copies, write the contents to local storage and to the clipboard

nodes.name: onDragFromToolbarToCreateShape
nodes.description:
/**
 * A helper method to use in {@link tldraw#TLUiToolItem.onDragStart}to create a shape by dragging it from
 * the toolbar.
 *

nodes.name: TldrawUiTranslationProvider
nodes.description: Provides a translation context to the editor.

nodes.name: useTranslation
nodes.description: Returns a function to translate a translation key into a string based on the current translation.

nodes.name: useDirection
nodes.description: Returns the current text direction ('ltr' or 'rtl') based on the current translation.

nodes.name: containBoxSize
nodes.description: Contains the size within the given box size

nodes.name: downsizeImage
nodes.description: Resize an image Blob to be smaller than it is currently.

nodes.name: getEmbedInfo
nodes.description:
Tests whether an URL supports embedding and returns the result. If we encounter an error, we
return undefined.

nodes.name: putExcalidrawContent
nodes.description: Put excalidraw clipboard content onto the current page.

structuredParams.name: point
structuredParams.description: The point at which to put the excalidraw content.

nodes.name: copyAs
nodes.description: Copy the given shapes to the clipboard.

structuredParams.name: ids
structuredParams.description: The ids of the shapes to copy.

nodes.name: exportAs
nodes.description: Export the given shapes as files.

structuredParams.name: ids
structuredParams.description: The ids of the shapes to export.

nodes.name: removeFrame
nodes.description: Remove a frame.

structuredParams.name: ids
structuredParams.description: Ids of the frames you wish to remove.

nodes.name: fitFrameToContent
nodes.description: Fit a frame to its content.

structuredParams.name: id
structuredParams.description: Id of the frame you wish to fit to content.

nodes.name: getTextLabels
nodes.description: Return all the text labels in a geometry.

structuredParams.name: geometry
structuredParams.description: The geometry to get the text labels from.

nodes.name: sanitizeSvg
nodes.description:
Sanitizes an SVG string by removing dangerous elements, attributes, and URIs
while preserving safe content including foreignObject (for text rendering),
style elements (for fonts with data: URLs), and animation elements.
Embedded SVG data URIs on `<image>`/`<feImage>` are recursively sanitized.

Returns the sanitized SVG string, or an empty string if the input was
malformed (parse error) or contained no safe content after sanitization.

nodes.name: pointsToBase64
nodes.description:
Helper function to convert draw shape points from VecModel[] to base64 string.
Uses delta encoding for improved Float16 precision.
This is useful for tests that create draw shapes with the legacy array format.
examples:
```ts
const segments = [{ type: 'free', path: pointsToBase64([{x: 0, y: 0, z: 0.5}]) }]
```

nodes.name: base64ToPoints
nodes.description:
Helper function to convert base64 string back to VecModel[] points.
Decodes delta-encoded points to absolute coordinates.
This is useful for tests that need to inspect draw shape points.

nodes.name: createDrawSegments
nodes.description:
Helper function to create draw shape segments from legacy array format.
This allows tests to use the old format while the shape uses the new base64 format.

nodes.name: renderHtmlFromRichText
nodes.description: Renders HTML from a rich text string.

nodes.name: renderHtmlFromRichTextForMeasurement
nodes.description: Renders HTML from a rich text string for measurement.

nodes.name: renderPlaintextFromRichText
nodes.description: Renders plaintext from a rich text string.

nodes.name: renderRichTextFromHTML
nodes.description: Renders JSONContent from html.

nodes.name: replaceTabsWithSpaces
nodes.description: Replace any tabs with double spaces.

structuredParams.name: text
structuredParams.description: The text to replace tabs in.

nodes.name: stripCommonMinimumIndentation
nodes.description: Strip common minimum indentation from each line.

nodes.name: convertCommonTitleHTMLEntities
nodes.description: Takes common HTML entities found in web page titles and converts them to regular characters.

structuredParams.name: text
structuredParams.description: The text to convert HTML entities.

nodes.name: stripTrailingWhitespace
nodes.description: Strip trailing whitespace from each line and remove any trailing newlines.

nodes.name: tryMigrateAsset
nodes.description:
We want to move assets over to our new S3 bucket & extract any relevant metadata. That process is
async though, where the rest of our migration is synchronous.

We'll write placeholder assets to the app using the old asset URLs, then kick off a process async
to try and download the real assets, extract the metadata, and upload them to our new bucket.
It's not a big deal if this fails though.
